mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at or near zero (below 0.25%) signals an accommodative monetary regime that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when FEDFUNDS remains in the zero lower bound.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.13% as of 2021-01-15 indicates zero-bound regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window during zero-bound periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering a negative equity risk premium adjustment that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 100k persons in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances following PAYEMS declines > 100k, or the correlation between monthly PAYEMS changes and subsequent 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real retail sales (RSAFS) over a 3-month rolling window signals robust consumer demand and earnings growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as equity valuations expand on improved profit forecasts.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.10) in periods of rising RSAFS compared to flat or declining RSAFS, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below its 6-month trend while the unemployment rate rises simultaneously, signaling stagflationary pressure. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days because stagflation compresses both growth and margin expectations.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth rate falls below 6-month moving average AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.7065 and UNRATE at 5.3% as of 2021-01-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite the joint INDPRO deceleration and UNRATE rise signal, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in a logistic regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 900k units annualized, signaling a contraction in residential investment demand and forward-looking economic weakness. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the housing sector contraction propagates to broader economic growth.
regime_hint: demand-side contraction or housing cycle downturn
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly lower (t-test p > 0.10) when HOUST < 900k compared to HOUST >= 900k, or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in more than 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1%, creating a negative real rate that signals monetary accommodation but also inflation risk. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive returns in early-cycle recovery but negative returns if inflation expectations become unanchored.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 237.031 and FEDFUNDS at 0.13% as of 2021-01-15
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with accommodative policy and emerging inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the mechanism's predictive direction reverses (becomes negative) in periods where inflation expectations rise above 2.5% as measured by breakeven inflation rates.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate falls below 5.0% while payroll employment growth accelerates above 150k persons per month, signaling a tight labor market and wage pressure that supports consumer spending and equity valuations. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5.0% AND monthly PAYEMS growth > 150k persons
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.3% and PAYEMS at 141842k as of 2021-01-15
regime_hint: tight labor market with strong wage growth and consumption support
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between the joint UNRATE-PAYEMS signal and subsequent 20-day returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) contract month-over-month, signaling a demand-side recession. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward across the equity market.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% AND RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16287.7B and RSAFS at 442032M as of 2021-01-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between GDPC1 growth and RSAFS changes is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in predicting forward equity returns.
